title: Diagnostic Narrative (M2.11)
description: Top-level synthesis of the 11 specialist-agent findings + simulation metrics + ranking into one diagnostic paragraph that opens the report. Phrased as an engineering hypothesis, never as a compliance claim.

title.description: Short heading for the diagnostic section (e.g. 'Input filter resonance dominates the conducted-EMI band').

narrative.description: 1-3 paragraph engineering narrative. Names the dominant issue, references at least one variant and one knowledge source (when available), proposes the next action. Hypothesis language only ('likely', 'suggests', 'requires verification') -- never 'will pass EMC'.

dominant_issue.description: One-sentence summary of the leading diagnostic conclusion.

cited_findings.description: Agent names whose findings most strongly support the narrative.

cited_variants.description: Variant labels referenced in the narrative (e.g. 'baseline', 'par-trace-L-iso-25x1-max').

cited_rule_ids.description: Rule_IDs from the retrieved snippets that support the narrative.

confidence.description: Synthesis confidence. Low when inputs are sparse or contradict each other.

llm_generated.description: True when an LLM call produced the narrative; false for the deterministic stub used under --llm none.

limitations.description: Caveats attached to the diagnosis (e.g. 'no layout', 'time-step too coarse for CISPR band').
